behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation), so a stubbed STT response would hide real misses (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the behavior text is scoped to "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels" (single agentic stage, not a multi-agent chain).
behaviors.test_description: Send a real multi-speaker meeting clip to the production STT API and assert returned transcript keeps speaker-separated utterances with stable labels that can be mapped to attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic on transcript text; the failure boundary is local function/object logic (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor applies). No real OS/network/subprocess or model judgment is required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures with explicit assignments (including edge phrasing) and assert exact structured outputs for assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model decisions about coverage/completeness of action items per attendee; deterministic mocks would always pass and miss true omission failures (LLM-DEP + deterministic-assertion trap). I considered Workflow, but rejected it because this behavior can be validated at the summarization decision boundary without requiring multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears at least once in action items.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets routing logic after decisions are already identified; this is local transformation/mapping logic. No real infra or model output quality is needed for this specific failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Call decision-routing function with pre-identified decision objects plus narrative content and assert decisions populate only the `decisions` schema field.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether the real model surfaces and places decisions correctly; mocked outputs hide extraction/placement quality failures (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). I considered Workflow, but rejected it because behavior text can be exercised with one real summarization step from transcript to summary.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts containing explicit decisions and distractor narrative, then assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and not only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple sequential segment-level model calls plus merge behavior; real failures emerge from cross-step handoff, accumulation, and coherence across steps (MULTI-STEP). Integration is insufficient because correctness is not only orchestration plumbing; it depends on chained agentic outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real long-audio pipeline (>60 min), verify segment boundaries and sequential processing occurred, then validate merged output preserves cross-segment decisions/action items without loss or contradiction.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly end-to-end and includes multiple AI-dependent stages (transcription + summarization) in sequence, so minimum boundary is full workflow. Lower levels miss inter-stage failures, retry/recovery interactions, and real-output-dependent progression.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like E2E run with a real meeting recording: upload, real STT, real summarization, and output delivery; assert successful completion and valid structured artifact without human intervention.
